cmes.note: A clear partial-halo CME seen in STEREO A COR2 to the SW, with the start of the event covered by the nighttime data gap in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph. The start of this event is likely much earlier. This CME in STEREO A likely (but not definitively) corresponds to one of several follow-up CMEs to the 2026-03-06T18:12Z CME, possibly to the front that is first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-07T00:00Z. The CME is also visible in to the SE in GOES CCOR-1.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative, approximate analysis with two coronagraphs, initially based on the fitting of the clear halo front in STEREO A COR2A and later assuming that one of the less clear follow up fronts in SOHO/CCOR1 is the corresponding front (likely the front first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-07T00Z), however this front is more of a speculation. The speeds in analysis can vary depending on the chosen half width, which in this analysis was based on the SOHO LASCO imagery and set to 37 degrees.

cmes.note: A faint and narrow CME front seen to the SE in COR2A. The source is likely far-sided, possibly indicated by the minor movement of field lines seen in GOES SUVI 195 on/behind the SE limb after 2026-03-07T09:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: This minor CME is only seen in STEREO A COR2 and not seen in other coronagraphs. The source is likely far-sided, therefore only plane-of-sky analysis is possible for this event.

cmes.note: A faint, narrow CME first seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-03-07T13:38Z. It is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 or SOHO LASCO C2/C3. A slow filament eruption on the SW limb in GOES SUVI 304 starting after 2026-03-07T09:30Z (and seen at the same time in STA EUV 304 close to the SW limb) was considered as a source candidate; however, there is too much time between this eruption and the start of the CME for this eruption to be the source. The source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: A loop-like front to the ENE that is originally seen in SOHO LASCO as the southern part of a messy strange front conglomerate (likely two combined fronts). It is not seen in STEREO A because of a nighttime data gap. It is seen the clearest in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is not clear.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the nighttime data gap in STEREO A and no clear source has been found for it, so it is likely far-sided. Only plane-of-sky analysis with GOES CCOR-1 is possible for this event.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-07T14:48Z. It is also seen to the NE in GOES CCOR-1. It is very faintly seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The front is the more northern portion of a two-part front seen in the NE. The more southern part is evaluated as CME: 2026-03-07T14:30Z. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-7T15:36Z. It is only seen in SOHO LASCO C2 and dissipates before it reaches the end of the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. It is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 or SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is no independent source signature for the feature. It is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-03-07T14:30Z and CME: 2026-03-07T14:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single coronagraph plane-of-sky measurement using running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the ENE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-03-07T17:24Z. It is also seen to the ENE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The front is very slow to start, so analysis is based on the propagation of the CME once it reaches the later frames in the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. There is some faint, minor, nearly continuous field line movement seen beyond the E limb in SDO AIA 171 for several hours before and after the start of the CME that could be related.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 starting sometime before 2026-03-07T18:54Z. This feature first becomes visible part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C3. It is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2. It is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2 and is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. There is not a good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided. This CME follows a very similar trajectory to earlier CME: 2026-03-07T13:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative plane-of-sky measurement based on limited frames from SOHO LASCO C3.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-03-07T23:00Z. It is also seen to the E in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3. It is covered by a data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is no source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated POS measurement using additional SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 frames.
Preliminary plane-of-sky measurement based on GOES CCOR-1 and early frames from SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and N/NE in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source appears to be a narrow moving/opening field lines visible along the NE limb in SDO/AIA 193 and 171 starting around 2026-03-08T09:29Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-08T14:48Z as well as by STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a small area of dimming and field line opening seen beginning at approx. 2026-03-08T13:39Z in SDO AIA 193. This eruption is located at approx. N15E40, but appears to exhibit some Northwestern deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-03-08T18:08Z, as well as by GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-08T16:12Z. The source of this event is a C2.0 flare and minor eruption seen in SDO AIA 131 and 304 peaking at 2026-03-08T15:07Z. This eruption was followed by a small dimming signature seen located at approx. N05E60 beginning at approx. 2026-03-08T17:04Z as seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2/GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: This faint CME is visible to the SE in GOES CCOR-1. It is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap. The source of this CME appears to be a filament eruption visible along the SE limb in GOES SUVI 304 and 284 starting around 2026-03-09T10:44Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky fit using GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a planned data gap. The source for this event is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures observed on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the Southeast in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-10T02:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-03-09T13:09Z to 2026-03-10T04:09Z. The source of this event is a small filament eruption seen along the SE limb (approx. S25E90) in GOES SUVI 304 with liftoff beginning at approx. 2026-03-10T01:57Z. A small region of field line opening is seen in association with this eruption in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on the approximate source location.
Plane-of-sky due to source along the limb and lack of STEREO A to triangulate. Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-10T04:48Z. This CME fades before exiting the field of view in SOHO LASCO C2 and is therefore not visible in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 or GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a small region of brightening with a small amount of ejecta seen in GOES SUVI 304 located at approx. N25E63 beginning at approx. 2026-03-10T04:17Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW by GOES CCOR-1 beginning at 2026-03-10T10:45Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. The source of this event was a far sided prominence eruption seen from beyond the NW limb in GOES SUVI 304. Field line opening is also visible in GUES SUVI 195 and 284. This eruption began at approx. 2026-03-10T07:30Z, but did not fully detach from the disk until approx. 2026-03-10T09:57Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-10T12:24Z. This front fades very quickly and is therefore not visible in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 or GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a C2.4 flare from Active Region 14389 (N13E70) peaking at 2026-03-10T12:04Z as seen in GOES SUVI 131, along with field line opening seen in GOES SUVI 193 and 284. It appears that this eruption mostly fails and falls back towards the solar disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NW in GOES CCOR-1, no observations in real-time due to a STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 data gap. The source for this CME is not directly observed, best observed as field line movement over the far-sided west limb starting around 2026-03-10T21:00Z in GOES SUVI 284. A second front at 2026-03-10T22:45Z in CCOR-1 is likely a slower bulk following an initial faster shock front, though without direct source observations and triangulation this is a somewhat speculative connection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Shock front plane-of-sky with CCOR-1 only, due to unobserved source longitude and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate.
Bulk front plane-of-sky with CCOR-1 only, due to unobserved source longitude and lack of STEREO A COR2 to triangulate.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery. This event is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 imagery and is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a data gap. The source may be related to an eruption observed on the southwest limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-03-11T09:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The source is a filament eruption centered around S27E57, observed starting around 2026-03-12T13:29Z in GOES SUVI 171/195/283/304 as the filament ejecta, moving field lines, and brightening on disk. Partially overlaps with far-sided CME 2026-03-12T15:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft 3D analysis using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to no available STEREO A COR2 to triangulate, using the on-disk source longitude of E57. With possible deflection, the longitude could range E50-E65.

cmes.note: CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 and faintly in GOES CCOR-1, too faint for LASCO C3 and not observed in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The source is likely far-sided, observed only in GOES SUVI 284 as field line movement over the east limb starting around 2026-03-12T14:20Z. Partially overlaps with on-disk CME 2026-03-12T14:36Z, and fades after the first few frames in LASCO C2.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky due to no STEREO A COR2 to triangulate, and CME front becoming too faint in C2 past the earliest couple of frames to resolve in other coronagraphs' running difference imagery.

cmes.note: CME seen to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and the southeast in STEREO A COR2. The CME is seen in STEREO A COR2 following a data gap from 2026-03-12T19:39Z to 2026-03-13T04:09Z. The CME is obscured by Earthshine and the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 imagery, but visible in later frames. The source is a filament eruption spanning from approximately S45E30 to S20W30 as seen in GOES SUVI 304 imagery with liftoff starting at around 2026-03-13T22:09Z. Dimming is also best seen in GOES SUVI 195 imagery. The eruption is not visible in available STEREO A EUV imagery due to the STEREO A data gap mentioned above.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery as well as the source location in SWPC_CAT. Limited STEREO A images were available for this measurement due to the data gap.

cmes.note: CME visible to the N/NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The CME overlaps in the field of view with CME: 2026-03-13T06:48Z in these coronagraphs. The CME is also visible to the NE in STEREO A COR2. There is at least one potential source eruption on the Earth-facing disk as seen in GOES SUVI 195 and 284 imagery, starting around 2026-03-13T05:44Z as dimming centered around N35W20. Additional dimming can be seen starting around the same time, centered around N25E20, and this is the more likely source candidate.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.

cmes.note: CME visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is far-sided, and opening field lines can be seen on the west limb in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery starting around 2026-03-13T04:55Z. An associated eruption cannot clearly be seen on-disk in STEREO A EUV imagery, and the west limb of the STEREO-A facing disk is at approximately longitude W143.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2. Not clearly visible in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, or STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source is an eruption from the vicinity of AR 14392 (approximately S15E60) seen as dimming in GOES SUVI 195 starting at 2026-03-13T09:16Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery based on source location.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Not clearly visible in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source is likely far-sided, as moving field lines can be seen beyond the west limb in STEREO A EUV imagery starting at 2026-03-13T13:25Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-13T17:38Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-13T15:09Z. The source of this CME is a small region of dimming and field line movement seen beginning at 2026-03-13T16:24Z located at approx. N45W10 in GOES SUVI 195, but appears to be heavily deflected to the North based on the opening direction of the field lines.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint, narrow CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-13T18:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and partially in GOES CCOR-1, as this CME is partially obscured by the pylon. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began at 2026-03-13T15:09Z. There is no clear source seen for this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of W90 due to lack of discernable source. Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-13T20:48Z but fades before exiting the field-of-view in C2 and is therefore not visible in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 or GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a C8.9 flare from Active Region 14392 (S15E53) which peaks at 2026-03-13T20:23Z. This flare can be seen in GOES SUVI 131, along with dimming and field line features seen in GOES SUVI 195 and 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
